name: Black Bean and Corn Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Dried black beans (picked over and rinsed)
30 oz Canned black beans (rinsed and drained)
2 Ears corn (kernals cut off the cob)
1 Avocado (peeled, stone removed, and cut into 1/2-inch pieces)
1 small Red bell pepper (seeded and cut into 1/2-inch pieces)
1 Fresh hot chile pepper (seeded and minced)
recipeInstructions:
If using dried beans:
1. Place the beans in a large bowl and add enough water to cover by 2 inches.
2. Place the bowl in a cool place and let the beans soak for 6 to 12 hours.
3. Drain and rinse the beans.
4. Put the beans into a large pot and add enough fresh water to cover the beans by 1 inch.
5. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat, reduce the heat, cover, and simmer until the beans are barely tender (1-1/2 to 2 hours depending on the age of the beans).
6. Thoroughly drain the beans and let them cool.
7. Put the lime juice, olive oil, garlic, salt, and cayenne in a small jar.
8. Cover with the lid and shake until the ingredients are well mixed.
9. In a salad bowl, combine the cooked or canned beans, corn, avocado, bell pepper, tomatoes, green onions, chile pepper, and cilantro.
10. Pour over the lime dressing and stir until well coated.
11. (The salad can be prepared a few hours ahead. Don't add the avocado until serving time. Refrigerate, and adjust the seasonings before serving.)
Makes 4 to 5 main-course servings or 8 to 10 side-dish servings.
